title: Password Fields
text: Password Fields In password fields, the content of the field is not displayed. To define a password field in a Form, set the field syntax to NDI (No Display) and use an Edit Box widget for the field. NDI is a static property and cannot be changed after the form is displayed. This means that it must be set before the ProcScript edit statement is executed. You can set the field syntax declaratively in the Syntax field of the field properties, or set it in ProcScript using the $fieldsyntax instruction. For example: $fieldsyntax(PASSWORD.PERSON.MYMODEL)="NDI" edit Copy If you want to prevent the field from being edited, you can also set the field syntax to NED (non-editable) or to NPR (no prompt). Thus, the following combinations all define a password field: NDI NDI , NED NDI , NPR A password field cannot be created with the HID (Hide) field syntax, (nor by setting NDI , NED , and NPR ). For example, you cannot create a hidden password field and un-hide in later in ProcScript. In this case. it will be a normal field. Balloon Tips On some Windows systems, if Caps Lock is on, a tooltip warning may be displayed on an NDI field. (It should not be displayed if the field is also set to NED or NPR .) Figure 1. Caps Lock Balloon Tip This depends on the value of the EnableBalloonTips variable in the registry key: HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Microsoft/Windows/Currentversion/Explorer/Advanced If EnableBalloonTips is set to 1 , the tooltip is displayed; if it is set to 0 , the tooltip is not displayed. Related Links Field Syntax Codes Field syntax codes specify the data entry pattern, the allowed characters, and whether fields are mandatory or read-only. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
